A Tcl script to enforce coding style rules for brace placement in source files.

# Purpose
The code is a script written in the Tcl programming language. It is designed to analyze source files and enforce specific formatting rules related to the placement of braces and type names in code structures such as `struct`, `union`, and `enum` definitions. The script iterates over a list of source file names obtained from the `getSourceFileNames` function and processes each file to check for compliance with these formatting rules.

The script uses a token-based approach to parse the source files. It retrieves tokens from each file using the `getTokens` function, which provides details such as the token value, line number, column number, and token type. The script maintains a state machine to handle different contexts, such as macro definitions and structure definitions. It checks that right braces and type names are on the same line in structure definitions and that braces are placed on separate lines from other code elements.

The script reports any formatting violations it detects by calling the `report` function with the file name, line number, and a message describing the issue. This functionality helps ensure that the source code adheres to a consistent style, which can improve readability and maintainability.
